name: Natural Extracts Industries (NEI)
revenue_source: RocketReach firmographic ($2M reported for 2025); ZoomInfo corroborates
staff_source: RocketReach (37 employees); LinkedIn hiring activity corroborates
existing_debt: DOB Equity (Dutch family-backed impact investor) — undisclosed equity stake; prior investment from other DFIs likely
has_audited_accounts: Unknown — B Corp status and impact investor backing suggest investor-reviewed financials
capital_use_of_funds: Farmer outgrower expansion, working capital for vanilla purchasing season, processing capacity
capital_need_source: Claude estimate based on scale and DOB Equity profile
crs_relationship: Indirect — Juan Guardado (co-founder) also co-founded NEI Uganda; NEI Uganda appeared in CRS VINES project context
crs_notes: NEI is the most investment-ready social enterprise in the Tanzania spice/vanilla space. DOB Equity backing provides credibility. Tanzania operations are legally distinct from Uganda entity — confirm structure before proceeding. Blue Pacific Flavors (USA) markets Kilimanjaro Vanilla via NEI supply chain. USAID Digital Agriculture Ecosystem Assessment (Tanzania) featured NEI. Strong livelihoods story: $250 incremental income per household target. Note: may be above SME ceiling if Tanzania + Uganda revenue consolidated.
sme_classification_note: Revenue likely within SME range for Tanzania entity alone; confirm consolidated vs. standalone financials
notes: Operates both 'NEI' B2B brand and 'Viva Vanilla' retail/consumer brand. Has own extraction facility in Tanzania — only regional company to do so. SourceTrace digital traceability system in use.

name: GFP Organics Ltd
primary_contact: Mr. Ayo (founder; full name not publicly listed)
farmer_network_size: 654 (120 of whom are youth); 1,704 hectares under certified organic management
capital_use_of_funds: Processing house expansion, working capital for harvest purchasing, organic certification maintenance
capital_need_source: Claude estimate based on organic processor profile and farmer network size
crs_notes: High alignment with CRS livelihoods and environment priorities. 20+ years organic certification is exceptionally strong for the sector. Women-led processing (98% female labour) is a standout feature. Supported by USAID Tuhifadhi Maliasili (wildlife/biodiversity conservation project) — confirms USAID touchpoint in-country. Community microfinance groups established with district government support — signals existing credit culture among farmer base. CEO contact not publicly listed; approach via website or TanTrade/TASPA introduction.
notes: Operations based in East Usambara Mountains, a globally recognised biodiversity hotspot. Organic from 2005 — one of Tanzania's longest-certified organic spice exporters. Registered as independent company in 2011 after years operating as informal scheme. Good Agricultural Practices training includes organic pesticides (neem, garlic, rabbit urine). Kwamhosi village processing house is a documented community anchor.

name: Makaru Agro Ltd
farmer_network_size: 1,000+ (advisory/extension); 31 contracted outgrowers; sources across Tanzania, Uganda, Zambia
revenue_source: MSU Alliance for African Partnership profile
capital_use_of_funds: Outgrower scheme expansion, processing plant scale-up (chilli powder), working capital for harvest
capital_need_source: Claude estimate based on company stage and outgrower model
crs_notes: Founder Abdul Mkono is a YIELD programme participant (MSU/Alliance for African Partnership — USAID-funded leadership programme for young agri-entrepreneurs). Seedstars Tanga winner (2018) — early-stage investor validation. Processing plant in pilot stage as of last reported period. EU chilli export standards are a certification gap to close. Outgrower model across three countries is ambitious for a company of this size — supply chain reliability risk. Chilli is not CRS's priority spice (vanilla/cloves/pepper) but is a legitimate adjacent crop.
notes: Founded 2014; chilli exports to Kenya began 2017. Drone and drip irrigation technology in use. AGRISTA app planned to link agricultural stakeholders — not confirmed as live. CEO has agricultural economics degree. Cross-border outgrower model (TZ/UG/ZM) is unusual for this scale.

name: Agri Promise Co. Ltd (Linda Spices)
farmer_network_size: Unknown — sources from Morogoro smallholder farmers
export_markets: Local (Mjimwema shop); online — export markets unconfirmed
crs_notes: Placeholder entry. Featured on Tanzania Spices website — good agricultural practices claimed. Brand name 'Linda Spices' (meaning 'secure' in Swahili). Morogoro is a cinnamon and black pepper production area. Requires direct outreach to assess readiness.
notes: Very early stage — established 2019. Limited public profile beyond Tanzania Spices listing. Uluguru Mountains sourcing is notable — the area has biodiversity significance.

name: Get Aroma Spices
farmer_network_size: Unknown — sources from Morogoro and Singida highlands
crs_notes: Placeholder entry. Featured on Tanzania Spices label website — climate-smart agricultural practices and farmer partnerships cited. Cardamom and ginger focus is relevant to CRS spice mandate. Very limited public profile. Requires direct outreach before further assessment.
notes: Founded 2019 alongside Agri Promise — two similar Morogoro-area processors at roughly the same stage. May be worth a combined outreach visit.

name: Halisi Organic Farm
hq_location: Zanzibar (sourcing across Zanzibar, Kilimanjaro, Usambara, Maasai, Hadzabe communities)
farmer_network_size: Multiple communities — Zanzibar, Maasai, Hadzabe, Uluguru, Kilimanjaro, Usambara
export_markets: Tourism / local retail; export readiness unconfirmed
staff_count_est: Unknown — women hand-packers from multiple communities
crs_notes: Strong gender and livelihoods story — products hand-packed by women from indigenous and rural communities. Carbon offsetting via solar energy cited. Reforestation partnerships mentioned. Currently tourism-facing (spice tour visitors) which limits export revenue visibility. Cross-community sourcing model is unusual and worth understanding. Requires direct outreach.
notes: Multi-community sourcing model spans mainland and Zanzibar — operationally complex for an early-stage enterprise. Solar energy use and reforestation are genuine environment credentials. Tourism channel is a current revenue floor but limits scale.

name: Buyuni Bees
farmer_network_size: Unknown — sources from Iringa and Tabora ginger farmers
crs_notes: Placeholder entry. Ginger specialist — Iringa and Tabora are confirmed ginger-growing regions. Airtight packaging cited as quality differentiator. Tanzania ginger exports are currently very low (<50 tonnes/year dried) — value-add processing is the right strategic position. Very limited public profile. Requires direct outreach.
notes: Ginger focus is strategically interesting — Tanzania has export potential in dried/processed ginger but current volumes are low. Multi-region sourcing (Mwanza, Iringa, Tabora) is a strength if supply chain is managed. Name suggests possible honey diversification.

name: Get Group Co. Ltd
certifications: Unknown — organic and GAP practices stated
crs_notes: Placeholder entry. Founded 2022 — very early stage. Featured on Tanzania Spices label website. GAP and sustainable practices stated but not verified. Requires direct outreach before any assessment is possible.
sme_classification_note: Too early stage for lending consideration without significant TA first
notes: Only 3 years old at time of research. Lowest priority for near-term lending engagement — included for pipeline completeness.

name: Green Crop Tanzania Ltd
farmer_network_size: Unknown — sources direct from local farmer cooperatives
capital_use_of_funds: Working capital for clove purchasing at harvest; cold storage or grading equipment
capital_need_source: Claude estimate based on clove exporter profile
crs_notes: Direct-from-cooperative sourcing model eliminates intermediaries — good value chain alignment. Clove is Tanzania's dominant export spice (90%+ of spice exports). Website is live and professional. Requires direct outreach for financial profile. Clove season working capital is the most common lending need in this segment.
notes: Clove exporter with working website and B2B focus. Direct cooperative sourcing is a positive livelihoods signal. GAP compliance is a step toward certification but not yet certified organic. Zanzibar clove export requires permit from Ministry of Agriculture, Natural Resources, Livestock and Fisheries.

name: Gando Farmers Association (GAFA) of Pemba
farmer_network_size: ~100 certified organic clove farmers (as of MOU signing period)
certifications: Organic (TanCert — pilot project with ZSTC and GANEFRYD, Denmark)
capital_use_of_funds: Harvest purchasing float, post-harvest processing (drying, grading), certification maintenance
capital_need_source: Claude estimate based on cooperative size and organic clove pilot scope
crs_notes: Pemba organic clove pilot is a documented success story — ~100 certified farmers, European buyer (GANEFRYD), MOU with ZSTC and Zanzibar Government. Organic premium positioning is exactly the kind of upmarket shift CRS's lending facility would want to support. GAFA may benefit from CRS's international buyer networks or certification cost financing. Key risk: ZSTC is the parastatal intermediary — need to understand GAFA's direct vs. ZSTC-mediated export relationship. Approach via TanTrade or ZSTC introduction.
sme_classification_note: This is a farmer association, not a registered company — legal entity structure for lending must be confirmed
notes: Pemba cloves are documented to have 15–18% essential oil content — significantly higher than global average. GANEFRYD stated European demand for organic cloves at 10,000 tonnes/year minimum. GAFA is the smallholder anchor of the pilot. ZSTC relationship is both an enabler (branding, infrastructure) and a potential constraint (parastatal intermediation of prices).

name: Tanzania Organic Products Ltd
certifications: Organic (stated in IFC/REPOA export strategy document)
crs_notes: Named in REPOA / EU-funded Tanzania export strategy report as a certified organic producer — one of very few named in that document. Multi-location operations (Zanzibar, Tanga, Kigoma) suggest meaningful scale. No web presence found — research via TOAM (Tanzania Organic Agriculture Movement) or TanCert recommended. Broad product range (ginger, pepper, turmeric, chillies, lemongrass) across multiple growing regions is a supply chain strength.
notes: Source: REPOA / Landell Mills 'Harnessing the potential of Tanzania's exports' report (EU-ACP TradeCom II). Certified organic is confirmed via that document. No independent web presence found — research via TOAM or TanCert directory recommended. Also cited alongside Clove Stem Oil Distillery (Pemba) and Evasa T (Arusha) as established organic operators.

name: Clove Stem Oil Distillery (CSOD)
capital_use_of_funds: Distillation capacity expansion, raw material (clove stems) purchasing
capital_need_source: Claude estimate based on essential oil processor profile
crs_notes: Essential oil distillation is a high-value-add processing step — clove stem oil commands strong international prices in fragrance, pharmaceutical, and food sectors. Pemba location means access to the highest-quality clove stems globally. Value-add model aligns with CRS facility's interest in processors with farmer network impact. Very limited public profile. Named in EU/REPOA export strategy report. Research via ZSTC or TanTrade introduction recommended.
notes: Source: REPOA / EU-ACP TradeCom II report. Clove stem oil has 72–90% eugenol content — used in dental anesthesia, fragrance, and food preservation. Pemba is the optimal sourcing location globally for this product. Distillery capital requirements are typically equipment-heavy — lending use case would likely be equipment or working capital for raw material.
